title: Biology and Life Sciences
description: RAND's research on biology and life sciences explores advances in biotechnology, genetics, and health sciences that influence medicine, public health, and national security. It provides evidence-based insights to guide policy on innovation, regulation, and emerging biological technologies.
text:
Europe Is Betting on Biotech—But Success Depends on Demand
The success of the European Biotech Act should ultimately be judged on whether it meets health policy goals and whether patients across Europe can access, afford, and benefit from new therapies.
Developing a Risk-Scoring Tool for Artificial Intelligence–Enabled Biological Design: A Method to Assess the Risks of Using Artificial Intelligence to Modify Select Viral Capabilities
A rise in artificial intelligence (AI) use in biology has driven transformative developments but could also pose dual-use risks. This report presents a multifaceted scoring system to assess the risks of using AI to modify select viral capabilities.
Measuring Biological Capabilities and Risks of AI Agents: Generating and Interpreting Evidence from Agentic Evaluations
This perspective examines biological agentic evaluations as a tool for assessing risks from increasingly autonomous AI systems and offers practical guidance on how evaluation design shapes conclusions about biological capability and risk.
Position: Human Baselines in Model Evaluations Need Rigor and Transparency: (With Recommendations & Reporting Checklist)
We argue that human baselines in foundation model evaluations must be more rigorous and more transparent to enable meaningful comparisons of human vs. AI performance, and we provide recommendations and a reporting checklist towards this end.
Bridging the Digital to Physical Divide: Evaluating LLM Agents on Benchtop DNA Acquisition
This report describes an automatable evaluation of eight frontier large language model (LLM) agents on their ability to design DNA segments, interact with a benchtop DNA synthesizer, and generate laboratory protocols.
Prioritizing Feasible and Impactful Actions to Enable Secure AI Development and Use in Biology
This paper provides policy and technology strategies collected from a diverse range of sources placed in the context of an organizing framework aligned with steps in the AI-enabled creation of a biothreat.
Information Hazards and Biotechnology
The working group focused on cases where information potentially enabled significant irreversible harm but for which the possibility also existed of limiting or delaying access to that critical information.
A Firebreak for Mirror Life
A commentary published in response to an article on mirror life published in the journal Issues in Science and Technology.
Governing the AI-biotech Convergence
Biotechnology manipulates living systems for benefits in medicine, agriculture, and more, but poses ethical, safety, and regulatory challenges due to dual-use concerns, cultural values, and rapid global scientific progress.
Ethical Criteria for Self-Identifying Societal Risk Associated with Dangerous Gain-of-Function Research
We suggest a layered review process for the assessment of societal consequences to inform implementation of the 5 May 2025 executive order "Improving the safety and security of biological research."
A Roadmap for Accelerating Therapies for Parkinson's Disease
The discovery of a gene pathway that contributes to Parkinson's risk has opened up a promising new avenue for developing therapies. Delivering a therapy that slows or prevents Parkinson's progression will require intentional and sustained collaboration across stakeholders.
